author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
No Man Ever Saved Save by Christ.
title: No Man Ever Saved Save by Christ.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 34 [XXIX.]—No Man Ever Saved Save by Christ.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now, whoever maintains that human nature at any period required not the second Adam for its physician, because it was not corrupted in the first Adam, is convicted as an enemy to the grace of God; not in a question where doubt or error might be compatible with soundness of belief, but in that very rule of faith which makes us Christians.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: How happens it, then, that the human nature, which first existed, is praised by these men as being so far less tainted with evil manners?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: How is it that they overlook the fact that men were even then sunk in so many intolerable sins, that, with the exception of one man of God and his wife, and three sons and their wives, the whole world was in God’s just judgment destroyed by the flood, even as the little land of Sodom was afterwards with fire?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: From the moment, then, when “by one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin, and so death passed upon all men, in whom all sinned,” the entire mass of our nature was ruined beyond doubt, and fell into the possession of its destroyer.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: And from him no one—no, not one—has been delivered, or is being delivered, or ever will be delivered, except by the grace of the Redeemer.
